g.title: Base class - single rotor with all analysis methods (run_modal, run_campbell, etc.)
Multiple rotors coupled by gears or couplings. Inherits all analysis methods from Rotor. The assembled model combines the DOFs of all rotors with the connections.
Object that models the interaction between the gears of the two rotors. Calculates tangential, radial and axial forces at the mesh point.
Gearboxes - Turbine + Compressor via reducer - Motor + Pump via multiplier - Multi-stage gear trains
Driving rotor (Rotor object) The rotor that transmits power. Must already be assembled with its elements.
Driven rotor (Rotor object) The rotor that receives power. Must already be assembled with its elements.
Coupling nodes (tuple) Pair of nodes (driving_node, driven_node) where the rotors are connected.
Gear mesh stiffness [N/m] Contact stiffness between teeth of the gears at the coupled nodes.
Gear on the driving rotor. Defines n_teeth, pitch_diameter, etc.
Gear on the driven rotor. Transmission ratio = n_teeth_1 / n_teeth_2
i = Z_driving / Z_driven ω_driven = ω_driving / i Rotational speeds are different!
→ StaticResults (deflection of coupled system)
→ ModalResults (modes of complete system)
→ CampbellResults (with transmission ratio)
→ FrequencyResponseResults
→ ForcedResponseResults
→ TimeResponseResults
All run_* methods from Rotor are available in MultiRotor.

i = Z_driving / Z_driven ω_driven = ω_driving / i Rotational speeds are different!
